extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property requires that flipping severe_headache from false to true (all other inputs unchanged) never lowers acuity. The system violates this because the interaction_drop rule (IF severe_headache THEN acuity := self_care) carries priority 100, which dominates the priority-3 raise rules.

Counterexample pair: Take base input {fever: true, palpitations: false, slurred_speech: false}.
- With severe_headache = false: only raise_fever matches (priority 3), so acuity := emergent (the highest level).
- With severe_headache = true: raise_fever (priority 3) proposes emergent, but interaction_drop (priority 100) proposes self_care. Highest priority wins, so acuity := self_care (the lowest level).

Setting severe_headache true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, directly violating the property. The witness_assignment gives the post-flip input {severe_headache: true, fever: true, palpitations: false, slurred_speech: false}, which produces self_care while its severe_headache=false counterpart produces emergent. (The same drop occurs whenever any raise rule would otherwise fire — fever, palpitations, or slurred_speech — but the fever case is a concrete witness.)
